articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Goal! TSG Hoffenheim 0, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1. Martin Terrier (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from the left side of the six yard box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Victor Boniface.Goal awarded following VAR Review.

Goal! TSG Hoffenheim 0, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2. Victor Boniface (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Granit Xhaka with a through ball.
Alexander Prass (TSG Hoffenheim) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! TSG Hoffenheim 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2. Mërgim Berisha (TSG Hoffenheim) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Andrej Kramaric with a cross.
Martin Terrier (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
Second Half begins TSG Hoffenheim 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2.
Robert Andrich (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Jeremie Frimpong replaces Nathan Tella.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Jacob Bruun Larsen replaces Mërgim Berisha.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Valentin Gendrey replaces Alexander Prass.
Goal! TSG Hoffenheim 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3. Florian Wirtz (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Goal! TSG Hoffenheim 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 4. Victor Boniface (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Jeremie Frimpong.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Tom Bischof replaces Tim Drexler.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Aleix García replaces Robert Andrich.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Amine Adli replaces Martin Terrier.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Patrik Schick replaces Victor Boniface.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Nordi Mukiele replaces Piero Hincapié.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Max Moerstedt replaces Marius Bülter.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Umut Tohumcu replaces Andrej Kramaric because of an injury.
Kevin Akpoguma (TSG Hoffenheim) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, TSG Hoffenheim 1, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 4.

article.headline: Leverkusen back to winning ways with Hoffenheim thumping
article.description: Champions Bayer Leverkusen cruised to a 4-1 victory at Hoffenheim on Saturday thanks to a dazzling performance by Victor Boniface that included two goals and an assist.
article.linkText: Boniface shines, Leverkusen back to winning ways

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (TSG Hoffenheim vs. Bayer Leverkusen - Game Highlights) 2024/09/14 ESHEET

article.story:
Champions Bayer Leverkusen cruised to a 4-1 victory at Hoffenheim on Saturday thanks to a dazzling performance by Victor Boniface that included two goals and an assist.

Leverkusen, who face Feyenoord at the start of the Champions League campaign next week, bounced back after losing to RB Leipzig just before the international break for what was their first domestic defeat in more than a season.

On Saturday they took control quickly and struck twice in the first half-hour, with Boniface twice beating the offside trap to give the visitors a 2-0 lead.

The Nigeria international first set up Martin Terrier for a tap-in after 17 minutes and then scored himself in the 30th.

Hoffenheim's Mergim Berisha, making his first appearances since a knee ligament tear last November, cut the deficit in the 37th minute and had another chances for a second goal a little later.

Leverkusen, however, never lost control of the game and restored their two-goal lead courtesy of a Florian Wirtz penalty in the 72nd minute.

Boniface then single-handedly dismantled the Hoffenheim defence with a strong run down the left, powering a shot past keeper Oliver Baumann in the 75th to lift Leverkusen to fourth place on six points.

"It was an important test for us to not concede a second goal after going 2-0 up," said coach Xabi Alonso, whose team had lost 3-2 at home to Leipzig after going 2-0 in front.

"We reacted well and this time we controlled the game better and were more stable until the end. We know we will get our chances if we are patient and that is what we have to patiently work towards."

Borussia Dortmund are top on seven points, with Bayern Munich, on six, visiting Holstein Kiel later on Saturday.
